news.summary: Auguste Escoffier School of Culinary Arts (Escoffier), based in Schaumburg, Illinois, disclosed on May 9, 2025, that it has been recognized as a top workplace for the sixth consecutive year by Great Place To Work®. As the leading culinary school brand in the United States, Escoffier has earned this certification, further solidifying its reputation as an exceptional employer. This achievement is a testament to the positive work environment cultivated at Escoffier, setting it apart from others in the industry. This accreditation by independent analysts underscores Escoffier's commitment to providing a supportive and rewarding atmosphere for its employees.
news.headline: Auguste Escoffier School of Culinary Arts in SCHAUMBURG, US, certified as a great workplace for the sixth year in a row.

news.summary: ANI Pharmaceuticals Inc (NASDAQ: ANIP) announced first-quarter adjusted earnings of $1.70 per share, surpassing last year's $1.21 and exceeding the expected $1.38 per share. The company's sales totaled $197.12 million, marking a 43.4% increase from the previous year and beating the projected $180.68 million. Excluding the Alimera acquisition, organic net revenue saw a 31.7% year-over-year growth. Net revenues for rare diseases, including Cortrophin gel, Iluvien, and Yutiq, soared by 86.7% to $69.0 million. Cortrophin Gel net revenues climbed 43.1% to $52.9 million due to increased volume. During the quarter, ANI Pharmaceuticals witnessed a surge in demand, recording the highest number of new patient starts and new cases initiated since launch. Cortrophin Gel experienced growth throughout the period.
news.headline: ANI Pharmaceuticals Inc reported Q1 earnings of $1.70/share, up from $1.21 a year ago, with sales of $197.12 million, up 43.4%. Cortrophin Gel net revenues increased 43.1% to $52.9 million. CEO notes strong demand and positioning against potential tariff impact.

news.summary: Pepe (CRYPTO: PEPE) has surged by 30%, leading to a significant increase in liquidations and whale activities within the cryptocurrency market. The meme coin is currently valued at $0.00001252 with a market cap of $5.26 billion, showing a 29.9% increase in the last 24 hours and a 42.7% rise over the past week. Meanwhile, Dogecoin (CRYPTO: DOGE) is priced at $0.2043 with a market cap of $30.5 billion, reflecting a 9.1% increase in the last 24 hours and a 12.3% increase over the last seven days. Shiba Inu (CRYPTO: SHIB) stands at $0.00001500 with a market cap of $8.8 billion, experiencing a 9.4% surge in the past 24 hours and a 10.7% increase over the last seven days. Notably, crypto trader MaxBecauseBTC has pointed out that Pepe is approaching a crucial resistance level at the 180-day VWAP, alongside a significant retest of the trend breakdown from February. He suggests that a breakout at this point could trigger a rapid price surge for Pepe. Traders are now closely monitoring the developments, with expectations of a highly active weekend for the meme coin.
news.headline: Pepe cryptocurrency surges 30%, surpassing Dogecoin and Shiba Inu in trading volume. Price: $0.00001252, Market Cap: $5.26 billion.

news.summary: In light of Wildfire Awareness Month, occurring in May 2025, Mercury Insurance is providing valuable tips to assist families in staying safe and prepared for potential evacuations. They emphasize the creation of a go-bag as a crucial step in readiness. This initiative aims to equip families with necessary knowledge in the event of wildfires, ensuring their safety and preparedness.
news.headline: Mercury Insurance provides tips for evacuations during wildfire season in recognition of Wildfire Awareness Month.

news.summary: Last night in New York, the Fashion Institute of Technology (FIT) presented a runway show highlighting the talent of its award-winning Fashion Design BFA graduates. The event featured a video of the runway show along with downloadable images. The annual event, known as the Future of Fashion show, displayed the exceptional creativity of the students at FIT.
news.headline: FIT hosted 2025 Future of Fashion runway show presented by Macy's, featuring award-winning Fashion Design BFA graduates.

news.summary: A healthcare company, AmeriStaff Nursing Services, based in Troy, Michigan, collaborated with Oakland University School of Nursing to support the 37th annual Nightingale Awards for Nursing Excellence. The event is a significant platform in Michigan for honoring outstanding nurses.

news.summary: As of March 31, 2025, the total number of voting rights and shares forming the capital has been disclosed. This information is critical for stakeholders as it provides insights into the governance structure and ownership of the company. Investors and analysts use this data to assess the company's performance and decision-making processes. Having transparency regarding voting rights and capital composition ensures accountability and can influence investment decisions. It is a way for companies to communicate with their shareholders and demonstrate financial integrity. These figures serve as key indicators of the company's financial health and market position.
news.headline: BIC: Total voting rights and shares as of March 31, 2025 disclosed.

news.summary: Celebrating 87 years of serving members and the outdoor community, the renowned specialty outdoor retailer REI Co-op in Seattle marks this milestone with its annual sale. The event, which signifies REI's commitment to outfitting members for their active lifestyles, is the largest sale of the year for the co-op. The sale offers various discounts and deals on outdoor gear and equipment, emphasizing the brand's dedication to supporting its members' adventures.
news.headline: REI's Anniversary Sale from May 16-26 celebrates 87 years, offering the biggest discounts for members.

news.summary: At the 30th Anniversary Awards Dinner of the National Police Defense Foundation in Rice Lake, Wisconsin on May 9, 2025, Anthony Imperato, the Founder and CEO of Henry Repeating Arms, pledged $60,000 to charitable causes supporting law enforcement. He also presented custom engraved rifles at the event.
news.headline: Henry Repeating Arms pledges $60,000 to law enforcement charities, presents engraved rifles at National Police Defense Foundation's 30th Anniversary.

news.summary: A report on Malaysia's social commerce market forecasts a 21.8% annual growth rate, with the market expected to reach US$1.36 billion by 2025. The report provides insights on social commerce trends across various sectors, operational KPIs, retail product dynamics, and consumer demographics. The market in Malaysia has seen strong growth trends.
news.headline: Malaysia's social commerce market to reach $1.36 billion by 2025, growing at 21.8% annually - ResearchAndMarkets.com.

news.summary: Compass, Inc. (NYSE: COMP) released first-quarter financial results that fell below expectations. The company reported a net loss of 9 cents per share, missing the analyst consensus of a 6-cent loss. Quarterly sales were disclosed at $1.36 billion, lower than the expected $1.42 billion. Robert Reffkin, Compass' Founder and CEO, highlighted the company's strong performance, citing a 7.3% increase in organic transactions and a 27.8% rise in total transactions. In comparison, the market saw a 2.1% decline in transactions, showcasing Compass' outperformance by approximately 9% in organic transactions and 30% in total transactions. Organic quarterly market share also experienced growth. More detailed information can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Compass, Inc. (NYSE: COMP) reports Q1 losses of 9 cents/share, missing estimates of 6 cents/share. Sales: $1.36B, falling short of $1.42B estimate.CEO mentions outgrowing the market with a 7.3% increase in organic transactions and a total transactions increase of 27.8% compared to a 2.1% decline in the market.

news.summary: On May 9, 2025, the U.S. Postal Service Board of Governors revealed their selection of David Steiner to serve as the 76th Postmaster General and CEO of America. Upon successful completion of background and ethics assessments, Steiner will assume the role, succeeding the former Postmaster.
news.headline: David Steiner appointed as 76th Postmaster General and CEO of USPS by Board of Governors in Washington.

news.summary: Navy Federal Credit Union is hosting the inaugural MAM JAM Concert Series in honor of Military Appreciation Month. The event will showcase Grammy-winning duo Dan + Shay in Cedar Park, TX (near Fort Cavazos) and Wilmington, NC (near Camp Lejeune). The concerts will take place on Saturday, May 17, 2025, at the H-E-B Center in Cedar Park, TX, and a venue in Wilmington, NC. This event aims to celebrate and recognize the military community through music and entertainment.
news.headline: Dan + Shay to headline Navy Federal's MAM JAM Concert Series for Military Appreciation Month with events near Fort Cavazos and Camp Lejeune.

news.summary: A recent report on the Australia telecoms industry predicts that the market will see a steady growth rate of 1.4% annually between 2025 and 2032. By the end of this period, the total market value is expected to reach USD 29 billion. This growth is notable given the economic challenges and inflationary pressures facing the country. The Australian telecommunications sector is projected to remain resilient due to its defensive characteristics, providing stability amidst turbulent economic conditions.
news.headline: The Australia Telecoms Industry Report foresees market growth at 1.4% annually from 2025-2032, reaching $29 billion by 2032.

news.summary: In the past 24 hours, approximately $777 million in short positions have been liquidated, largely due to the surging prices of Ethereum and Bitcoin. Ethereum specifically has been the primary contributor to this significant financial impact as it has experienced a notable increase in value.
news.headline: Cryptocurrency liquidations exceed $1.1B as Bitcoin, Ethereum, Solana prices surge. $777M shorts crushed in 24 hours, Ethereum leading the way.

news.summary: The recent report revealed that the current unemployment rate stands at 4.5%, with over 10 million individuals currently unemployed. Job growth has been stagnant over the past few months, with only 50,000 new jobs added to the market. The economy continues to face challenges as the GDP growth rate remains at a low 1.8%. Moreover, the inflation rate has increased by 2.5% causing concerns among consumers. The stock market also experienced a decline, with the main indexes dropping by 3% in the past week. Analysts predict a slow recovery in the upcoming months as the global economic uncertainty persists.
news.headline: US farm economy feeling impacts of Trump tariffs for the first time.

news.summary: In Santiago, Chile, Cencosud revealed its financial performance for Q1, disclosing consolidated revenues of CLP 4,031,583 million, marking a 2.4% rise from the previous year. Despite facing challenges due to 2024 being a leap year with Easter festivities in March, the company achieved growth. Net Income for the quarter stood at CLP 126,442 million, a significant turnaround from the CLP 601 million net loss in the equivalent period in the previous year.
news.headline: Cencosud in Q1 reports net income of CLP 126,442 million, with a 2.4% revenue increase to CLP 4,031,583 million.

news.summary: In a recent update, it was reported that there has been a significant increase in the number of COVID-19 cases in the past week. A total of 10,000 new cases were recorded, marking a sharp rise from the previous weeks. The surge in infections has put a strain on hospitals, with 500 new hospitalizations reported. Additionally, there have been 50 new deaths attributed to the virus in the same time period. Authorities are urging the public to adhere to safety measures such as wearing masks and practicing social distancing to curb the spread of the virus.

news.summary: Kyndryl, a renowned provider of vital enterprise technology services, made an important announcement today regarding leadership changes. Xerxes Cooper is set to assume the role of Global Leader of Kyndryl Delivery, overseeing crucial operations across various regions. This rotation impacts key positions in Delivery, Practice, and Country roles within the company. Known for its mission-critical services, Kyndryl's announcement marks a significant shift in its executive team. This decision comes with Kyndryl's stock listed as KD on the New York Stock Exchange.
news.headline: Kyndryl to rotate leadership roles, Xerxes Cooper to lead Delivery globally.

news.summary: This year's National Small Business Week Virtual Summit in 2025 showcased a series of complimentary educational workshops and panels tailored to address the challenges encountered by small business owners. Held in Washington on May 9, 2025, this summit attracted over 20,000 attendees who participated as registrants.
news.headline: 2025 National Small Business Week Virtual Summit, with over 20,000 registrants, offered workshops & panels for entrepreneurs.

news.summary: In Cambridge, Massachusetts on April 29, 2025, a surprising strategy has been revealed through research published in MIT Sloan Management Review. Despite consumer budget constraints during economic downturns, launching a new product could prove advantageous. This highlights the potential benefits of introducing innovative products during challenging economic times.
news.headline: Launching new product during a recession can be beneficial per MIT study. Consumers spend less during downturns.

news.summary: Appian Corporation (NASDAQ: APPN) has announced impressive first-quarter results, surpassing expectations and increasing its FY25 guidance. The company disclosed a 11% year-on-year revenue growth to $166.43 million for the quarter, exceeding the projected $163.35 million. Moreover, the adjusted EPS of 13 cents outperformed the anticipated three cents. Appian is anticipating second-quarter fiscal 2025 revenue to range between $158 million and $162 million, showing an 8%-11% Y/Y increase as opposed to the $162.16 million consensus. The projected adjusted EPS for the same period is expected to be $(0.15)-$(0.11), while analysts had estimated $(0.13). Looking ahead, for fiscal 2025, Appian foresees revenue of $680 million-$688 million, reflecting a 10%-12% Y/Y rise from the previous $680 million–$684 million estimate.
news.headline: Appian beats Q1 estimates with $166.43 million revenue, raises FY25 guidance. Projects 8%-11% revenue growth for Q2.

news.summary: Kenya aims to bolster government finances by implementing higher payroll taxes in order to ensure operational continuity and meet obligations to foreign creditors. This move is part of a broader strategy to stabilize the country's financial situation and address mounting debts.
news.headline: Kenya faces high debt, increasing taxes on workers to fund government and repay foreign creditors.

news.summary: Trustee Emeritus Hugh Durden has supervised approximately three decades of asset growth that has been advantageous for Nemours Children's Health and patients in Delaware and Florida. The Alfred I. duPont Charitable Trust, renowned for its generosity, has been a benefactor of this growth.
news.headline: Alfred I. duPont Charitable Trust appoints new chairman and trustee after Trustee Emeritus oversaw 30 years of asset growth.

news.summary: American Integrity Insurance Group, Inc. disclosed the completion of its first public offering, selling 6,875,000 shares of common stock at $16.00 per share. The stocks started trading on NYSE on May 8, 2025, using the symbol “AII.” Out of the total shares offered, 6,250,000 were from American Integrity, and 625,000 were from current stockholders.
news.headline: American Integrity Insurance closes initial public offering with 6,875,000 shares at $16/share, trading as "AII" on NYSE.
